desires: Secure the absolute maximum grade on tomorrow's midterm to cement my academic standing, regardless of how much it depletes my remaining reserves.
Avoid any confrontation or awkwardness with Mina and Jae by cancelling plans silently rather than explaining why I am too exhausted to show up.

goals.description: Execute the final consolidation of midterm notes and solve three complex practice problems to lock in a perfect score before sleep, treating this as the only non-negotiable investment for future academic leverage.
goals.rationale: My achievement priority (0.5) dictates that securing the maximum grade is the sole metric of success right now. The risk of failure outweighs any social obligation; maintaining my reputation as the reliable high-performer requires immediate cognitive expenditure on the exam, even if it means depleting my remaining energy reserves.

goals.description: Send a brief, vague text to Mina canceling our plans without offering an explanation or expressing guilt, framing the absence as a necessary 'strategic pause' rather than admitting exhaustion.
goals.rationale: As an avoidant individual with low assertiveness (3), I cannot handle the friction of negotiating a cancellation or explaining my fatigue. A silent, non-committal withdrawal minimizes interpersonal conflict while preserving the illusion of control over my schedule. This aligns with my tendency to prioritize long-term payoff (exam success) over short-term social repair.

agent.last_reasoning: I must cancel with Mina to preserve my energy for the midterm. A vague 'strategic pause' avoids the friction of explaining fatigue, aligning with my avoidant style and high academic priority.
